mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and imminent recession risks, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances following the trigger, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns during tightening cycles, or the signal reverses direction when CPIAUCSL exceeds 5.0%, indicating supply constraints dominate.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 315.0, inflationary pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing equity risk premiums and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL breaches the threshold, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in > 60% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, providing a fundamental floor for equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals weakening construction activity and reduced consumer confidence, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances following the trigger, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of low mortgage rates.
